subject: [PATCH RFC v3 01/19] mm: thread user_addr through page allocator for cache-friendly zeroing

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) pointed out that vma_alloc_folio() should be the only external interface used with a user address, and suggested deferring the addition of another interface for frozen folios until it's needed for hugetlb
reviews.raw_body:
Exactly, that's why I am saying that vma_alloc_folio() is the only external
interface people should be using with a user address. all other _noprof helpers
are supposed to be internal.

For hugetlb, we might need another interface for frozen folios later, which is
why I suggest to defer that.

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that he understands the reviewer's point about passing __GFP_ZERO and post_alloc_hook() handling zeroing, but still sees value in adding two paths into the buddy for cache-friendly zeroing.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah I follow now - we're of the same mind on all this then.

~Gregory

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch proposes a change to how the page allocator handles zeroing of memory, specifically for cache-friendly zeroing. It involves passing user addresses through the page allocator and using a wrapper function to handle cacheline alignment on the way out. However, after exploring this approach, it was deemed that the increased complexity may not be worth the benefits.
